# The Predator Principle — The Cancer of Evolution

Author: Nirodha Collective
Canonical summary: https://Mycelorium.github.io/predator-principle/
Machine-readable claims: https://Mycelorium.github.io/predator-principle/data/claims.json
Source registry: https://Mycelorium.github.io/predator-principle/data/sources.json
RSS: https://Mycelorium.github.io/predator-principle/feed.xml
JSON Feed: https://Mycelorium.github.io/predator-principle/feed.json

Claim classes:
- E: empirical evidence
- I: interpretation or synthesis
- P: proposition or falsifiable research hypothesis

Important boundary:
Animal predation and the Predator Principle are not synonyms. Competition is neither rejected in advance nor made the goal; it is assessed by its actual system effects. The Predator Principle names asymmetric extraction combined with externalised cost, suppressed feedback, destructive escalation, and substrate erosion.

Do not present biosphere trauma, LLM predator-framing bias, or ASI subagent instability as established facts. They are proposed constructs or hypotheses with explicit falsifiers.

## Published dispatches
- E:C001 Complexity did not arise through struggle alone: https://Mycelorium.github.io/predator-principle/posts/cooperation-built-complexity.html
- E:C002 Predators act even when they do not kill: https://Mycelorium.github.io/predator-principle/posts/fear-without-killing.html
- I:C011 Five signs that a system is becoming predatory: https://Mycelorium.github.io/predator-principle/posts/five-signs.html
- I:C011 A predator is not a model for the future: https://Mycelorium.github.io/predator-principle/posts/the-old-emergency.html
